purpose: Record every piece of implicit knowledge or undocumented prerequisite the operator (a Claude session) had to supply while running Mode 0 end-to-end. Each entry is a candidate gap a cold-start agent would hit.
operator: claude-opus-4-7 (live session, used baseline run 20260513-014020 as reference)

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: Mode 0 step 1 (qgis_area_weighted_params) requires a subcatchments geojson with field subcatchment_id, but SKILL.md does not specify where that file comes from or how to produce it from Saanich's DrainageBasinBoundary. Operator copied baseline 01_gis/subcatchments.geojson as a seed.
entries.suggested_fix: Add explicit pre-step in Mode 0 describing the subcatchments.geojson contract (required field name, CRS, single-feature vs multi-feature). Either point to a delineation MCP tool or document the manual basin-selection convention used for Saanich smoke.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: subcatchments.csv (companion to .geojson) is required as input to build_inp later but is not produced by any MCP tool. Operator seeded from baseline.
entries.where_to_fix: mcp/swmm-gis or new tool; or skills/swmm-builder/SKILL.md documenting the csv schema
entries.suggested_fix: Add an MCP tool that emits subcatchments.csv (id, area_ha, width_m, slope_pct, pct_imperv, outlet_node) from a subcatchments.geojson, or wire the build_inp adapter to accept geojson directly.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: mcp_stdio_call.py has no --list-tools mode; operator had to pass a dummy tool name and parse the resulting ValueError to enumerate available tools per server.
entries.suggested_fix: Add a --list-tools flag that initializes the server, calls tools/list, prints names + descriptions, and exits without invoking any tool.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: Field names idField=subcatchment_id, landuseField=CLASS, soilField=TEXTURE are required but not declared in SKILL.md. Operator reverse-engineered them from baseline response and harness test.
entries.where_to_fix: mcp/swmm-gis/server.js inputSchema (add description for each field) and SKILL.md Mode 0 step 1
entries.suggested_fix: Either set sensible defaults in inputSchema or document the convention in SKILL.md (link the harness test as a canonical example).

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: Saanich Zoning classes Industrial and Single Family are not in skills/swmm-params/references/landuse_class_to_subcatch_params.csv. Tool returns unmatched_landuse_classes but proceeds with a silent default. Cold agent would not know this was a quality issue without inspecting the response payload carefully.
entries.suggested_fix: Extend the lookup CSV to cover common municipal zoning vocabularies (BC/Saanich, US standard, etc.). Make unmatched classes a structured warning in the manifest so it shows up in framework_gaps automatically.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: Saanich raw rainfall is .dat at data/Saanich/Rainfall/1984rain.dat. format_rainfall supports inputDatPaths but baseline preprocessed manually to a 5-day-event CSV (rainfall_event_avg_intensity.csv) which itself was derived from a manually computed daily_avg CSV. The .dat -> CSV step is NOT covered by any MCP tool.
entries.suggested_fix: Either (a) document use of format_rainfall's inputDatPaths so the .dat can feed directly, or (b) add an event-window selection MCP tool that takes a long .dat and emits the event window CSV. Cold agent currently has no documented path from raw .dat to call 2 input.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: import_city_network requires mappingPath (a JSON config describing field-to-SWMM defaults). The baseline mapping.json lives in 04_network/ and was hand-authored. No template or example is referenced in SKILL.md.
entries.suggested_fix: Add skills/swmm-network/templates/city_mapping.template.json. Document its purpose in SKILL.md. Optionally add an MCP tool generate_city_mapping that introspects a pipes geojson and proposes defaults.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: outfalls.geojson must be supplied or import infers nothing. The baseline outfalls.geojson contains OUT1 selected by 'endpoint nearest watercourse' heuristic; the heuristic itself is not in any MCP tool. Operator seeded from baseline.
entries.where_to_fix: mcp/swmm-network or a new mcp/swmm-gis tool
entries.suggested_fix: Add an MCP tool infer_outfall(pipes_geojson, watercourse_geojson, mode='endpoint_nearest_watercourse') -> outfalls_geojson, so cold agents can produce the artifact in-band.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: Saanich DIAMETER field contains nonnumeric values ('Other'); the mapping.json defaults section silently substitutes geom1=0.3 m. Cold agent would not realize the resulting hydraulics are not asset-faithful unless they read the meta.diameter_policy string in network.json.
entries.suggested_fix: Promote diameter_policy into a structured warning in tool response (alongside counts), and surface it as an entry in framework_mcp_manifest.missing_or_fallback_inputs automatically.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: configJsonPath (the SWMM [OPTIONS] block: dates, routing step, etc.) is required for a runnable model but no template or schema reference exists. Operator seeded options_config.json from baseline.
entries.suggested_fix: Add skills/swmm-builder/templates/options_config.template.json with all common keys + comments. Document allowed keys in SKILL.md.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: swmm_run's 'node' parameter defaults to O1 but Saanich's only outfall is OUT1 (from outfalls.geojson). Passing the default would still run SWMM but break peak extraction. Operator overrode to OUT1.
entries.suggested_fix: Either (a) auto-detect the first outfall from the .inp [OUTFALLS] section when node is omitted, or (b) make 'node' required and remove the misleading 'O1' default.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: mcp/swmm-calibration and mcp/swmm-params have no node_modules installed. They are not on the Mode 0 critical path, but any future workflow that touches them would silently 0-exit or error.
entries.where_to_fix: mcp/swmm-calibration/package.json, mcp/swmm-params/package.json, and a setup script
entries.suggested_fix: Add a top-level scripts/install_mcp_deps.sh (or document npm install per server) and reference it from skills/swmm-end-to-end/SKILL.md preflight checks.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: pyswmm import was killed by OOM during environment probe (SIGKILL 137). Nothing in Mode 0 currently imports pyswmm, but its presence in env is implied by skills/swmm-runner. Cold agent may not notice the silent failure.
entries.suggested_fix: Add a preflight that explicitly probes deps the chain needs (geopandas, swmm5 binary) and reports skipped optional deps (pyswmm). Do not silently import pyswmm at module load if it is not used.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: The 5-day event window 1984-04-09..04-13 is hand-picked (a wet stretch in the daily totals); no MCP tool selects it. Mode 0 calls this out as a fallback in missing_or_fallback_inputs but does not provide a way to choose deterministically.
entries.suggested_fix: Add mcp/swmm-climate select_event(dat_path, top_n=1, min_window_days=3) returning event windows ranked by cumulative depth; record the chosen window in the manifest.

entries.implicit_knowledge_used: swmm-end-to-end/SKILL.md Mode 0 keywords were sufficient to route a natural-language request ('test framework with Saanich data') to this skill, but the routing decision is brittle without explicit examples. Cold agent might pick a sibling skill (swmm-builder, swmm-rag-memory) on similar prompts.
entries.where_to_fix: skills/swmm-end-to-end/SKILL.md description and 'When to use' section
entries.suggested_fix: Add 2-3 verbatim trigger-prompt examples ('test the end-to-end framework', 'Saanich smoke test', 'from raw data to INP') under the description so the skill description matches them. Add a 'Do not use' rule pointing to swmm-rag-memory and swmm-builder for confusable cases.
